title: Tavita's Touch
intro: Tavita is my wife. She throws every piece by hand — and finishes it with a precision most machines can't match. The work was already exceptional. What it needed was an identity that said so before a word was spoken.

blocks.label: THE DIFFERENCE
blocks.thesis: That precision is rare by hand. It's what makes her work hers.

blocks.label: THE ANCHOR
blocks.thesis: Tavita is my wife. Her perfectionism at the wheel became the strategy.

blocks.label: THE MARK
blocks.thesis: Two T's, like hands closing around clay — set in a thin serif that doesn't forgive mistakes.

blocks.label: COLOR
blocks.thesis: Charcoal and cream, pulled from the clay. Restraint, not decoration.

blocks.label: THE STAMP
blocks.thesis: A heavier mark for the pieces. Turn one over — the brand is in the material.

blocks.label: THE SYSTEM
blocks.thesis: One mark, built to stay itself for years. Recognition compounds.
